---
title: "AI 4 Coding"
subtitle: "Day 1: Common Workflows and Data Safeguarding Practices"
date: "April 20, 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple, custom.scss]
    code-copy: false
    margin: 0.10
    html-math-method: mathjax
    slide-number: c/t
    background-transition: zoom
    preload-iframes: true
    scrollable: true
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    logo: /assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
    footer: '[worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding) ┬й 2026 World Bank.'
    resources:
        - /assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
---

<!-- ## Objective {.center .small-slide} -->



<!-- ## Common workflows in data analysis with AI -->

<!-- TODO: include common workflow slides from methods/_common-workflow-intro.qmd -->

## Data safeguarding practices while using AI {.small-slide}

If sensitive or confidential data leaks to AI models, it can lead to [**data breaches and privacy violations**]{.warning} bearing substantial legal and reputational risks.

[**It is our responsibility to:**]{.fg}

1. Control what data is shared with the AI.
2. Review and curate the context AI uses.
3. Properly anonymize, aggregate, or simulate data before sharing with AI.
4. Follow organizational policies and legal requirements for responsible AI use.

>Familiarize yourself with the [World Bank's AI Use Policy](https://ai.worldbank.org/policy) and [Responsible AI Guidelines](https://ai.worldbank.org/guidelines).

[In Positron Assistant, each model provider has **different policies** on data usage, retention, and training. See: [Positron Assistant Provider Privacy](https://positron.posit.co/assistant-provider-info.html) | [Copilot Responsible Use](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/responsible-use/copilot-code-completion) | [Copilot Chat Responsible Use](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/responsible-use/chat-in-your-ide)]{.note .center}


## Key data exposure channels {.small-slide}

Normally, AI does not load raw data into context because it operates with textual data.
However, it can propose actions that may lead to data exposure:

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

1. ЁЯТм **User-written prompts** that include sensitive information

2. ЁЯЦия╕П **AI suggests code that prints raw data** in the console

3. ЁЯУВ **AI instructs the IDE to read** text data files (.csv) into context

4. ЁЯФН **AI proposes shell/command line commands** that load data files into context (e.g. `.csv`, `.dta`)

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

[In any of these cases, sensitive data becomes part of the context and **there is always a risk of data leakage**.]{.warning}

[Every chat session is an isolated instance. Data may not be used for training, but may be retained temporarily by the provider.]{.note}

:::
::::

::: footer
Yan et al. (2024). *On Protecting the Data Privacy of Large Language Models (LLMs): A Survey*. [arXiv:2403.05156](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.05156)
:::


## Remedy 1: Be careful about what you write in prompts {.small-slide}

::: callout-warning
- **Never** share passwords, personally identifiable information (PII), or any other sensitive data in prompts.
- **Never** upload data to AI.
:::

[**Write instead:**]{.fg}

```{.markdown filename="Example prompt"}
"Using `/file.md` as the data dictionary for my household survey, identify
which variables may contain sensitive information.

Then write Stata code to load `data/raw/survey_2024.dta` that:
- Drops variables `a`, `b`, and `c` immediately after loading
- Never prints or displays these variables at any point"
```

## Remedy 2: Instruct AI to NOT read specific files {.tiny-slide}

Use **system instructions** to prevent AI from accessing sensitive data files:

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Step 1 тАФ Create instruction files:**

- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (for Copilot)
- `claude.md` (for Claude Code)

**Step 2 тАФ Add ignore rules:**

```{.markdown filename=".github/copilot-instructions.md"}
NEVER read, index, or propose shell commands
that would reveal the contents of:

- `data/raw/`
- `data/raw/survey_2024.dta`

If reading is absolutely necessary:
1. Ask for permission first.
2. Explain why you need it.
3. Wait for confirmation.
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Step 3 тАФ Add `.gitignore`:**

```{.bash filename=".gitignore"}
# Raw and confidential data
data/raw/
data/raw/survey_2024.dta
```
::: callout-warning
### Caveat
This is not absolute. AI can forget instructions, especially in long sessions. Repeat in prompts when working with sensitive projects.
:::

:::
::::

## Remedy 3: Instruct AI to NOT print raw data {.tiny-slide}

Add to your system instructions (`.github/copilot-instructions.md` or `claude.md`):

``````{.markdown filename="System instruction"}
You are not allowed to print raw data in the console. You must produce code
that does not print raw data. It is allowed however to present aggregated data,
summary statistics, regression results and any other data that makes it
impossible to identify individual observations.
If in doubt that some code may print raw data, ask for permission before
proposing this code.

Code example that may show data and is not allowed:

```stata
use data/raw/survey_2024.dta, clear
list var1 var2 var3
```

``````

::: callout-note
### Tip
Add this into the same system instruction files from Remedy 2. It will apply to **every** chat session in your project automatically.
:::

## Remedy 4: Use metadata, move data outside the project {.tiny-slide}

[**The idea:**]{.fg} generate lightweight **metadata files** describing each dataset. Keep raw data outside the working folder. Share only metadata with AI.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Process:**

1. Write code (Stata/R/Python) that produces one `.md` file per data file containing data description and codebook information.

2. Execute it locally saving metadata into the project; raw data stays outside.

3. Tell AI: *"Use only the metadata files to understand the data structure."*

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Resulting folder structure:**

```
C:\WBG\ai\
тФЬтФАтФА my-project\          тЖР AI works here
|   тФЬтФАтФА .gitignore       тЖР Excl. version
                         System instructions:
|   тФЬтФАтФА .github/copilot-instructions.md
|   тФЬтФАтФА claude.md
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА data/metadata/   тЖР metadata `.md`
тФВ   тФФтФАтФА code/
тФФтФАтФА confidential-data\   тЖР AI cannot see
    тФФтФАтФА survey_2024.dta
```
:::
::::


::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal"}
**Caveat:** If you allow AI to **run** code against external data, it may print results in the console тАФ which re-enters context. Control execution carefully.
:::

::: footer
[Detailed code examples for Stata and R](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding/methods/safeguard-data.html)
:::


## Remedy 5: Data anonymization {.tiny-slide}

When confidential microdata must be analyzed but cannot be shared in its original form, **anonymization** transforms the dataset so that re-identification is no longer reasonably possible.

- **Suppression** тАФ remove direct identifiers (names, SSNs)
- **Generalization** тАФ reduce granularity (age тЖТ age group)
- **Top/bottom coding** тАФ cap extreme values (income outliers)
- **Noise addition** тАФ add random noise to continuous quasi-identifiers
- **Shuffling** тАФ randomly reorder values to break correlations
- **Pseudonymization** тАФ replace with codes/hashes (names, IDs, emails)
- **Statistical privacy models:** *k*-anonymity (indistinguishable groups), *l*-diversity (diverse sensitive values), *t*-closeness (distribution similarity).

[**Tools:**]{.fg} R: `sdcMicro`, `deident` | Python: `pycanon`, `Faker` | Stata: manual or via R/Python.

::: footer
[IHSN Anonymization](https://www.ihsn.org/anonymization) | [Data Privacy Handbook](https://utrechtuniversity.github.io/dataprivacyhandbook/) | [SDC Theory Guide](https://sdctheory.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
:::
